2604.23947.title: GamED.AI: A Hierarchical Multi-Agent Framework for Automated Educational Game Generation
2604.23947.paper_summary: We introduce GamED.AI, a hierarchical multi-agent framework that transforms instructor-provided questions into fully playable, pedagogically grounded educational games validated through formal mechanic contracts. Built on phase-based LangGraph sub-graphs, deterministic Quality Gates, and structured Pydantic schemas, GamED.AI supports two template families encompassing 15 interaction mechanics across spatial reasoning, procedural execution, and higher-order Bloom's Taxonomy objectives. Evaluated on 200 questions spanning five subject domains, the system achieves a 90% validation pass rate, 98.3% schema compliance, and 73% token reduction over ReAct agents (${\sim}$73,500 $\rightarrow$ ${\sim}$19,900 tokens/game) at $0.46 per game. Within this model configuration, these results suggest that phase-bounded architectural structure correlates more strongly with alignment quality than prompting strategy alone. Our demonstration lets attendees generate Bloom's-aligned games from natural language in under 60 seconds, inspect Quality Gate outputs at each pipeline phase, and browse a curated library of 50 games spanning all 15 mechanic types.
2604.23947.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹 GamED.AI，一個層級多代理框架，將講師提供的問題轉換為完全可玩、以教學為基礎的教育遊戲，並通過正式的機械合約進行驗證。該框架基於基於階段的 LangGraph 子圖、確定性的質量門和結構化的 Pydantic 模式，GamED.AI 支持兩個模板系列，涵蓋 15 種互動機制，涉及空間推理、程序執行和高階布魯姆分類法目標。在涵蓋五個學科領域的 200 個問題上進行評估，該系統實現了 90% 的驗證通過率、98.3% 的模式合規性，以及相較於 ReAct 代理的 73% 令牌減少（${\sim}$73,500 $\rightarrow$ ${\sim}$19,900 tokens/game），每個遊戲的成本為 $0.46。在這種模型配置中，這些結果表明，階段限制的架構結構與對齊質量的相關性比單純的提示策略更強。我們的演示讓參與者在 60 秒內從自然語言生成與布魯姆對齊的遊戲，檢查每個管道階段的質量門輸出，並瀏覽涵蓋所有 15 種機制類型的 50 款遊戲的策展庫。
2604.23947.author: Shiven Agarwal et.al.
2604.23947.authors: Shiven Agarwal, Yash Shah, Ashish Raj Shekhar, Priyanuj Bordoloi, Vivek Gupta
